architecture.summary: The system combines task-conditioned sparse autoencoders (SAEs) for concept discovery in language model latent space with differentiable structure learning (DAGMA-style) to recover a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of causal dependencies between sparse, interpretable latent features. Information flows from LLM activations into SAEs to identify concepts, then a structure-learning module infers causal edges between these concepts. The learned graph guides interventions to measure causal effects.

biblio.title: Causal Concept Graphs in LLM Latent Space for Stepwise Reasoning

claims.statement: CCG achieves Causal Fidelity Score of 5.654±0.625 on ARC-Challenge, StrategyQA, and LogiQA with GPT-2 Medium across five seeds (n=15 paired runs)
CCG outperforms ROME-style tracing (3.382±0.233) on Causal Fidelity Score
CCG outperforms SAE-only ranking (2.479±0.196) on Causal Fidelity Score
CCG outperforms random baseline (1.032±0.034) on Causal Fidelity Score
Difference between CCG and random baseline is statistically significant with p<0.0001 after Bonferroni correction
Learned graphs are sparse (5-6% edge density), domain-specific, and stable across seeds
Causal Concept Graphs capture learned causal dependencies between concepts in language model latent space for multi-step reasoning
Graph-guided interventions induce larger downstream effects than random interventions
